title: Water Worlds are orbital-only for construction
claim_text: Water Worlds in the current repository evidence should be treated as having no surface construction slots

title: Station economy is inherited
claim_text: Colony station economy should not be treated as a direct player-selectable value

title: One weak Refinery link was insufficient in the tested Wregoe case
claim_text: A single weak Refinery link did not restore Steel Titanium or Aluminium in the documented Dodec case

title: A4 post-build facility set included two High Tech Hubs
claim_text: The Rocha Liberty evidence set shows A4 after the intended High Tech support build completed

title: Rocha Liberty showed strong High Tech and Industrial support after build
claim_text: Rocha Liberty displayed strong Industrial x2 and High Tech x2 links plus multiple weak links in the post-build state

title: Rocha Liberty exposed mixed economies after build
claim_text: Rocha Liberty advertised Industrial High Tech Extraction Military and Refinery in the post-build state

title: Rocha Liberty showed multiple High Tech-style commodities after build
claim_text: Post-build evidence showed Computer Components Auto-Fabricators Robotics Micro Controllers and Advanced Catalysers among visible commodities

title: Raven projected deficits and placeholder plans were visible
claim_text: Raven planning screenshots showed unmet build-point needs and placeholder-style planned or unbuilt facilities

title: Apollo and Artemis Orbis previews looked mechanically identical on shown bars
claim_text: No visible colony-effect bar differences were observed between Apollo and Artemis Orbis layout previews

title: No reliable station-class superiority was shown in the preview evidence
claim_text: The available Orbis Ocellus and Dodec previews did not demonstrate clear mechanical superiority on the shown bars

title: A4 High Tech promotion is expected to broaden total material coverage
claim_text: The current A4 experiment predicts that raising High Tech to second place may improve whole-colony construction-material coverage

title: Absent primary artifacts are only indirectly represented
claim_text: The repository does not yet contain the underlying Mega Guide spreadsheet and flowchart artifacts and therefore cannot claim direct page-level extraction from them

title: Agriculture strong-link boosts
claim_text: Agriculture strong links are boosted by: Orbiting an Earth like world / Orbiting a Water World / [NOTE: PRESENTLY BUGGED - NOT WORKING] On or orbiting a terraformable body / On or orbiting a body with organics.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Strong Links
planner_implication: Prefer bodies and systems with these properties when you need stronger Agriculture routing.

title: Agriculture strong-link penalties
claim_text: Agriculture strong links are decreased by: On or orbiting an icy body / On or orbiting a planet that is tidally locked to its star / On or orbiting a moon that is tidally locked to its planet and its subsequent parent planet(s) are tidally locked to the star.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Strong Links
planner_implication: Avoid these properties when you need reliable Agriculture strong-link shaping.

title: High-security systems widen the none portion of faction security sliders
claim_text: The guide's compared systems show that higher security produces a wider 'none' section on faction security sliders, indicating slider shape varies with overall system security.

planner_implication: Do not treat security slider widths as fixed across differently developed systems.

title: Orbital Station Moves Away at High Speed - Unable to Dock
claim_text: Orbital Station Moves Away at High Speed - Unable to Dock: Under certain conditions the frame of reference for orbital construction sites is incorrect, and such sites become impossible to reach Workaround: Avoid building around closely-orbiting moons and/or binary bodies in close proximity to each other and/or large stars and non-main-sequence-stars. Tracker: https://issues.frontierstore.net/issue-detail/75280.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0

title: List of commanders who completed station missing.
claim_text: List of commanders who completed station missing.: CMDR’s contribution isn’t correctly reported in ports bulletin board Workaround: Learn to live with a bruised ego for the time being. Tracker: https://issues.frontierstore.net/issue-detail/74618.

title: "You are in danger" quitting delay shown when landed on construction site
claim_text: "You are in danger" quitting delay shown when landed on construction site: Logging off at construction sites triggers the 15-second “in danger” countdown timer Workaround: Wait out the timer, or log out elsewhere. Tracker: https://issues.frontierstore.net/issue-detail/73254.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0

title: Terraformorable modifier for agriculture not working
claim_text: Terraformorable modifier for agriculture not working: Planets which are “Candidates for Terraforming” should boost agriculture - but they presently don’t Workaround: Take the missing modifier into consideration when building. Tracker: TBD.

title: Agriculture Planetary ports bug out / produce no agri goods
claim_text: Agriculture Planetary ports bug out / produce no agri goods: Occasionally planetary (but not orbital) ports can “bug out” and produce no agricultural commodities at all Workaround: Focus on orbital agricultural facilities for the time being. Tracker: TBD.

claim_text: Vista Genomics and Pioneer Supplies are always available: Requirements for Vista Genomics and Pioneer supply to “activate” in certain facilities do NOT apply - they are always active. Workaround: Just be aware that one day the requirements may be fixed.. Tracker: TBD.

title: Primary port is permanent after completion
claim_text: Once built, the primary port can never be changed; it can only be cancelled or abandoned before completion.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: How To Architect A System? Construction Points, And A Tool You Will Love
planner_implication: Lock primary-port choice before committing large deliveries.

title: Tier 1 facilities generate yellow CP
claim_text: Tier 1 facilities cost no construction points and generate Tier 2 yellow construction points.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: How To Architect A System? Construction Points, And A Tool You Will Love
planner_implication: Use Tier 1 facilities to open Tier 2 construction.

title: Tier 2 facilities generate green CP
claim_text: Tier 2 facilities cost yellow construction points and generate green construction points.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: How To Architect A System? Construction Points, And A Tool You Will Love
planner_implication: Use Tier 2 facilities to unlock Tier 3 construction.

title: Tier 3 facilities are terminal for CP generation
claim_text: Tier 3 facilities cost green construction points and generate no further construction points.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: How To Architect A System? Construction Points, And A Tool You Will Love
planner_implication: Avoid consuming green CP until your T3 sequencing is settled.

title: Large settlements convert yellow CP efficiently
claim_text: Large settlements are the exception among Tier 2 facilities because they cost one yellow CP and generate two green CPs.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: How To Architect A System? Construction Points, And A Tool You Will Love
planner_implication: Use large settlements when farming green CP efficiently.

title: T2/T3 CP escalation begins at construction start
claim_text: T2 and T3 port CP escalation begins as soon as construction is initiated, not when the port is completed.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Before You Start: CP-Cost Increases For Tier 2 And Tier 3 Ports
planner_implication: Do not start extra high-tier ports casually because the later ones immediately become more expensive.

title: Primary port is exempt from T2/T3 escalation count
claim_text: The primary port does not count toward the escalating T2/T3 port cost limit.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Before You Start: CP-Cost Increases For Tier 2 And Tier 3 Ports
planner_implication: A T3 primary port effectively grants a free high-tier anchor outside later escalation math.

title: T1 ports are exempt from T2/T3 escalation
claim_text: Tier 1 ports are exempt from T2/T3 port CP escalation, as are installations, settlements, and hubs.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Before You Start: CP-Cost Increases For Tier 2 And Tier 3 Ports
planner_implication: Use T1 ports and non-port facilities when you need additional slots without inflating high-tier port CP costs.

title: First two T3 ports need at least six supporting facilities each
claim_text: The guide states that the first two T3 ports each require at least six supporting facilities to generate enough green CPs.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Before You Start: CP-Cost Increases For Tier 2 And Tier 3 Ports
planner_implication: Budget a large support footprint before promising multiple T3 ports.

title: Fifth T3 port needs at least thirty supporting facilities
claim_text: The guide states that a fifth T3 port requires at least thirty supporting facilities before it can be constructed.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Before You Start: CP-Cost Increases For Tier 2 And Tier 3 Ports
planner_implication: Treat very high port-count systems as support-facility-heavy capital builds.

title: Build T3 ports before T2 ports
claim_text: The guide recommends building T3 ports before T2 ports because T3 cost escalates by 6 each step while T2 cost escalates by 2.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Before You Start: CP-Cost Increases For Tier 2 And Tier 3 Ports
planner_implication: Sequence T3 ports first in any ambitious build plan.

title: T1 civilian planetary port is the only exempt large-pad colony facility
claim_text: The Tier 1 civilian planetary port is described as the only large-pad colony-type facility exempt from the T2/T3 port escalation mechanic.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Before You Start: CP-Cost Increases For Tier 2 And Tier 3 Ports
planner_implication: Use T1 civilian ports as escalation-safe large-pad anchors when appropriate.

title: Maximum concurrent builds is five
claim_text: A system can have up to five facilities under construction at the same time.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: How To Effectively Plan For System Construction
planner_implication: Schedule construction waves in batches of five or fewer.

title: Medium settlements often dominate small settlements
claim_text: The guide recommends caution with small settlements because medium settlements cost only modestly more but usually provide better benefits.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: How To Effectively Plan For System Construction
planner_implication: Prefer medium settlements unless slot pressure or prerequisite routing specifically favors small ones.

title: Top two economies no longer cannibalize each other’s supply
claim_text: Patch 4.2.0.1 changed markets so that goods produced by the top two economies are no longer consumed by ports or settlements linked to that economy.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: How To Effectively Plan For System Construction
planner_implication: Plan around one or two primary economies rather than broad multi-economy mixtures.

title: Third and lower economies still create cannibalization pressure
claim_text: The guide recommends minimizing influences from third-and-lower economies because they still introduce demand and supply cannibalization.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: How To Effectively Plan For System Construction
planner_implication: Treat third-and-lower economies as deliberate tradeoffs rather than harmless extras.

title: Contraband strong links require orbital facilities
claim_text: Contraband market strong links can only be created using orbital facilities, specifically pirate bases.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: The Value Of Space Versus Ground Slots
planner_implication: Reserve orbital slots if contraband shaping matters.

title: Insulating Membranes are orbital-only
claim_text: The guide names Insulating Membranes as a commodity restricted to orbital starports.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: The Value Of Space Versus Ground Slots
planner_implication: A pure-ground system cannot cover all refinery-adjacent build materials.

title: CMM Composites are planetary-only
claim_text: The guide names CMM Composites as a commodity restricted to planetary sites.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: The Value Of Space Versus Ground Slots
planner_implication: A pure-orbital system cannot cover all construction materials.

title: Refinery specialization requires the ground-only Refinery Hub
claim_text: The guide states that refinery markets can only be specialized using the ground-exclusive Refinery Hub.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: The Value Of Space Versus Ground Slots
planner_implication: Any serious refinery plan needs suitable ground slots.

title: Only T2/T3 orbital ports change interiors
claim_text: Only Coriolis, Ocellus, and Orbis ports can change interiors based on economy.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Station Interiors
planner_implication: Do not expect interior feedback from outposts or planetary ports.

title: Station interiors update weekly
claim_text: Port interiors update only on weekly maintenance.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Station Interiors
planner_implication: Do not use immediate interior appearance as real-time confirmation after a build change.

title: Interior economy uses the highest proportion with alphabetical tie-break
claim_text: When multiple economies are tied, station interiors use the first economy in alphabetical order.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Station Interiors
planner_implication: Alphabetical ties can mislead if you infer market dominance from visuals alone.

title: Specialised orbital ports have baseline values 0.5 or 1.0
claim_text: Specialised ports are assigned a baseline economic strength of 0.5 for scientific and industrial ground ports or 1.0 for several orbital specialised variants.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: How Do Colony-Type Ports Gain Economies? Base Inheritable Economy + Modifiers
planner_implication: Model specialised ports with their fixed baseline before adding links.

title: Specialised ports ignore local body inheritable economy
claim_text: Specialised ports are not affected by the local body's base inheritable economy.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: How Do Colony-Type Ports Gain Economies? Base Inheritable Economy + Modifiers
planner_implication: Do not choose a body for a specialised port expecting body inheritance to rescue it.

title: Specialised ports ignore local body economy modifiers
claim_text: Specialised ports are not affected by local-body inheritable economy modifiers such as rings, biologicals, or geologicals.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: How Do Colony-Type Ports Gain Economies? Base Inheritable Economy + Modifiers
planner_implication: Keep rings, organics, and geologicals out of specialised-port body projections.

title: Colony-type ports replace economy from body plus modifiers
claim_text: Colony-type ports replace their economy with the combination of local-body base inheritable economy and applicable inheritable modifiers.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: How Do Colony-Type Ports Gain Economies? Base Inheritable Economy + Modifiers
planner_implication: Treat colony-type ports as body-driven before link effects.

title: Local inheritable modifiers do not stack
claim_text: The guide states that local-body inheritable modifiers do not stack with base planet economies and do not stack with each other.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: How Do Colony-Type Ports Gain Economies? Base Inheritable Economy + Modifiers
planner_implication: Do not double-count multiple inherited modifiers without direct confirmation.

title: Geologicals are distinct from volcanism
claim_text: Geologicals are DSS/system-map body features and are distinct from volcanism, which instead modifies strong links.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: How Do Colony-Type Ports Gain Economies? Base Inheritable Economy + Modifiers
planner_implication: Keep inheritable-economy modifiers separate from strong-link modifiers.

title: Strong links form only within a local body
claim_text: Strong links are formed within facilities around a local body only.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: What Are Strong & Weak Links? How Are They Modified By Link Modifiers?
planner_implication: Use same-body facilities for deliberate market shaping.

title: Strong link base values scale by facility tier
claim_text: Strong links have base values of 0.4 for Tier 1, 0.8 for Tier 2, and 1.2 for Tier 3 before modifiers.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: What Are Strong & Weak Links? How Are They Modified By Link Modifiers?
planner_implication: Higher-tier support facilities are stronger economy shapers per slot.

title: Weak links are always 0.05
claim_text: Weak links have a fixed value of 0.05 per weak link.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: What Are Strong & Weak Links? How Are They Modified By Link Modifiers?
planner_implication: Treat weak links as spillover, not equivalent substitutes for strong links.

title: Weak links are never modified
claim_text: Weak links are not subject to any modifier, ever.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: What Are Strong & Weak Links? How Are They Modified By Link Modifiers?
planner_implication: Do not apply body or system modifiers to weak-link math.

title: Each supporting facility forms at most one strong link
claim_text: At most one strong link can form between a supporting facility and a port.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Strong Links
planner_implication: Multi-port local bodies split or reroute support rather than duplicating it.

title: Supporting facilities without a local port form no strong links
claim_text: If there is no port in the same local body as a supporting facility, that facility forms no strong links.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Strong Links
planner_implication: Orphaned facilities only contribute weak links across the system.

title: Strong-link boosts and penalties stack additively
claim_text: The guide says strong-link modifiers stack additively.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Strong Links
planner_implication: You can compound multiple boosted conditions when designing same-body support.

title: Strong links cannot fall below 0.1
claim_text: The guide states that strong-link strength cannot be decreased below 0.1.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Strong Links
planner_implication: Penalty bodies still retain some same-body shaping power.

title: Weak links reach all non-local-body ports
claim_text: Every non-port facility with an economy generates weak links to all ports outside its local body.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Weak Links
planner_implication: System-wide weak spillover is unavoidable once a facility exists.

title: Multiple same-body ports are usually a bad idea
claim_text: The guide's direct recommendation is that building more than one port on or around a given local body is generally a bad idea.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Special Case: Port-to-Port Strong Links
planner_implication: Default to one-port-per-body unless you are deliberately exploiting a niche routing behavior.

title: Highest-tier or earliest-built port wins port-to-port strong-link priority
claim_text: When multiple ports are present, the highest-tier port is linked to; if same tier, the first-built port is selected.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Special Case: Port-to-Port Strong Links
planner_implication: Build order matters when nesting ports around the same body.

title: Planetary facilities route strong links through the planetary port first
claim_text: If both planetary and space ports are present, planetary facilities strong-link to the planetary port, which then passes those links to the orbital port following the same priority rules.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Special Case: Port-to-Port Strong Links
planner_implication: Nested surface-orbital pairs can reroute support in non-obvious ways.

title: Planetary port may pass inherent economy upward to orbital port
claim_text: The guide says planetary ports currently appear to pass the planet's inherent economy upward to spaceports above them, and warns this is likely a bug.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Special Case: Port-to-Port Strong Links
planner_implication: Do not build around this behavior unless you are prepared for it to be fixed.

title: Lower-tier or later-built same-body ports convert to supporting facilities
claim_text: When multiple ports exist in a local body, lower-tier or later-built same-tier ports convert to supporting facilities.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Special Case: When Ports Convert To Being Supporting Facilities
planner_implication: Nested-port designs create support facilities rather than multiple independent destinations.

title: Converted ports emit weak links and are shielded from incoming weak links
claim_text: Converted ports form strong links with the local main port, emit weak links outward, and are shielded from incoming weak links from the rest of the system.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Special Case: When Ports Convert To Being Supporting Facilities
planner_implication: Use converted ports for protected mission or economy niches when necessary.

title: Anarchy systems hard-lock security at none
claim_text: For anarchy factions, the security slider is hard-locked at none.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Security
planner_implication: Do not attempt to engineer high security in anarchy systems.

title: Low security enables Interstellar Factors
claim_text: Low security makes an Interstellar Factors contact appear at local stations.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Security
planner_implication: Low-security builds can deliberately enable Interstellar Factors.

title: Wealth effect is still unknown
claim_text: The guide explicitly states that it does not know what wealth affects.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Wealth
planner_implication: Treat wealth as a tracked but presently weakly explained stat.

title: Standard of living effect is still unknown
claim_text: The guide explicitly states that it does not know how standard of living affects the system.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Standard of Living
planner_implication: Do not overfit planner recommendations to standard-of-living values yet.

title: Tech level 35 enables shipyards
claim_text: Technology Level 35 is the minimum for shipyards to come online.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Technology Level
planner_implication: Track TL35 as the shipyard threshold.

title: Tier 2 and Tier 3 ports grant Tech Level 35 immediately
claim_text: Building a Tier 2 or Tier 3 port immediately grants Tech Level 35.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Technology Level
planner_implication: Do not spend extra slots chasing TL35 if a T2/T3 port already solves it.

title: Tier 1 space outposts cannot have shipyards
claim_text: Tier 1 space outposts cannot have shipyards, period.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Technology Level
planner_implication: Do not recommend shipyard-dependent roles to T1 space outposts.

title: Development level affects commodity volumes and soft caps around 20
claim_text: Development level 0-20 affects the volume of available and demanded commodities and appears to soft-cap at 20.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Development Level
planner_implication: Track development level when estimating supply and demand growth.

title: Happiness affects weekly architect credits
claim_text: Happiness changes the amount of weekly credits paid to the system architect.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Happiness
planner_implication: Do not confuse system score with final payout because happiness shifts the result.

title: Top two economies have protected supply
claim_text: The first and second-ranked economies at a port have supply protected from being netted out by demand from other economies present at that port.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Which Economies Are Compatible With Each Other?
planner_implication: Keep desired producer economies in the top two positions if you need guaranteed output.

title: Top two economies do not have protected demand
claim_text: Demand from the top two economies can still be netted against the supply of any other non-top-two economy.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Which Economies Are Compatible With Each Other?
planner_implication: Protected top-two status prevents losing supply, not creating immunity from downstream demand.

title: Two-economy mixes are generally safe
claim_text: The guide states that two economies can be mixed without negative repercussions because top-two economies tolerate each other.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Which Economies Are Compatible With Each Other?
planner_implication: Design around one or two dominant economies wherever possible.

title: Same-strength rank order is believed alphabetical
claim_text: The guide marks same-strength economy rank ordering as to-be-confirmed and believes it is alphabetical.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Which Economies Are Compatible With Each Other?
planner_implication: Treat alphabetical tiebreak behavior as provisional.

title: Population grows weekly and follows a hybrid curve
claim_text: Population grows on weekly maintenance ticks and follows a hybrid formula that uses fixed values for the first 14 ticks and a logistic curve thereafter.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: What Does Population Affect, And How Do I Grow It?
planner_implication: Model population as a time curve instead of a linear climb.

title: Facility output scales with the square root of population
claim_text: Commodity supply and demand scale with the square root of population.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: What Does Population Affect, And How Do I Grow It?
planner_implication: Population growth gives diminishing returns on output.

title: Stations reach twenty percent of max population in about fifteen to sixteen days
claim_text: A station reaches about 20% of max population and about 44.72% of max population-driven production in 15-16 days after construction.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: What Does Population Affect, And How Do I Grow It?
planner_implication: Short-term bootstrapping happens fast even if full maturation takes a year.

title: Stations take about a year to approach asymptotic population
claim_text: A station takes about a year to approach its asymptotic population and production.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: What Does Population Affect, And How Do I Grow It?
planner_implication: Long-run optimization differs from short-run construction support.

title: Orbis production materially exceeds Coriolis production
claim_text: The population-driven production of a Coriolis is 18.11% that of an Orbis under otherwise equal conditions.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: What Does Population Affect, And How Do I Grow It?
planner_implication: Large orbital ports dominate long-run market volume.

title: Tier 1 planetary ports can exceed Coriolis population
claim_text: At K population 48 million, Tier 1 planetary ports vastly outclass Tier 2 Coriolis starports in population terms.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: What Does Population Affect, And How Do I Grow It?
planner_implication: Do not underestimate T1 planetary ports as high-population assets.

title: Population affects only the facility it belongs to
claim_text: Population increases production only for the applicable facility; system-wide population does not automatically raise output on unrelated facilities.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: What Does Population Affect, And How Do I Grow It?
planner_implication: Do not average system population across facilities when estimating output.

title: Higher population improves emissions grade
claim_text: Population increases the grade of emissions, such as shifting from low-grade to high-grade emissions.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: What Does Population Affect, And How Do I Grow It?
planner_implication: Population can matter for material farming beyond commodity output.

title: Higher population makes BGS control harder to swing
claim_text: Population makes changing BGS control of the system require more effort.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: What Does Population Affect, And How Do I Grow It?
planner_implication: Population-heavy systems are harder to manipulate politically.

title: Demolishing facilities may not reduce population
claim_text: The guide flags anecdotal reports that demolishing facilities does not reduce system population accordingly.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: What Does Population Affect, And How Do I Grow It?
planner_implication: Treat demolition-based population pruning as unverified.

title: Population is not part of system score
claim_text: Population is not a factor in a system's score.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: System Score And Credits Per Week
planner_implication: Score-focused builds do not need population-heavy facilities unless they also serve another goal.

title: Weekly architect credits equal score times about ten thousand times one plus happiness
claim_text: The weekly payout formula is System Score × about 10,000 credits × (1 + Happiness).
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: System Score And Credits Per Week
planner_implication: Estimate payouts from score and happiness rather than intuition.

title: Capital systems should start with a T3 primary port
claim_text: Appendix A recommends starting capital systems with a T3 primary port to gain a free high-tier station that does not consume later CP escalation budget.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Appendix A: Advanced Strategies
planner_implication: Use a T3 primary port for serious long-run capital systems.

title: Flipping economy order can matter more than boosting raw values
claim_text: Appendix A says that changing economy rank order can matter more than simply boosting a target economy's absolute value.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Appendix A: Advanced Strategies
planner_implication: Model rank-order objectives, not just raw percentages.

title: Large settlements can be farmed for T3 CP then replaced
claim_text: Appendix A recommends using large ground settlements to farm green CP and later deconstructing them after the desired T3 ports are built.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Appendix A: Advanced Strategies
planner_implication: Temporary CP farms are a valid but risky capital-build tactic.

title: Nested ports can create industrial or refinery strong links in space
claim_text: Appendix A says nested ports can create industrial or refinery strong links in space by converting a lower-tier port into a supporting facility.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Appendix A: Advanced Strategies
planner_implication: Use nested ports to manufacture same-body link patterns that ordinary slot layouts would block.

title: Nested ports can create protected mission spawners
claim_text: Appendix A says nesting ports can create protected converted ports that are shielded from incoming weak links and can be used to target mission types.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Appendix A: Advanced Strategies
planner_implication: Mission-focused planners may deliberately isolate converted ports.

title: Icy bodies are poor farms
claim_text: Case Study #2 says icy bodies and rocky-ice bodies make poor agricultural builds because they start with industry and carry agricultural strong-link penalties.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Case Study #2: Building An “Ideal” Agriculture System
planner_implication: Avoid icy bodies for agriculture unless forced.

title: Water worlds make the best space farms
claim_text: Case Study #2 says water worlds make the best space farms in the game.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Case Study #2: Building An “Ideal” Agriculture System
planner_implication: Prefer water-world orbital agriculture when available.

title: Rocky-ice worlds are elite refinery bodies
claim_text: Case Study #1 describes rocky-ice worlds as some of the finest refinery bodies in the game.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Case Study #1: Building An “Ideal” Refinery System
planner_implication: Prioritize rocky-ice worlds for refinery-led construction systems.

title: Agricultural planetary ports can bug out and show no agri goods
claim_text: The agriculture case study warns that planetary agricultural ports can occasionally produce no agricultural goods at all.
source_document: Elite Dangerous Colonization Mega Guide v2.3.0
section_heading: Case Study #2: Building An “Ideal” Agriculture System
planner_implication: Prefer orbital agriculture if you need safer output until the bug is resolved.

title: Steel is the most common major construction material
claim_text: Steel is required for all installations and often represents 25-30% of total mass.
source_document: AetherWave Colonisation Reference Tables (DaftMav-derived)
section_heading: Resource Requirements for Major Materials
planner_implication: Track steel first when planning bulk logistics.

title: CMM Composite is a major high-volume material
claim_text: CMM Composite is critical for large structures and typically represents 15-20% of total mass.
source_document: AetherWave Colonisation Reference Tables (DaftMav-derived)
section_heading: Resource Requirements for Major Materials
planner_implication: Keep both orbital and planetary sourcing in mind for CMM-heavy plans.

title: Aluminium is a core structural material
claim_text: Aluminium is listed as an essential structural component at roughly 10-15% of total mass.
source_document: AetherWave Colonisation Reference Tables (DaftMav-derived)
section_heading: Resource Requirements for Major Materials
planner_implication: Refinery coverage remains strategically important for aluminium-heavy programs.

title: Titanium is a major durability material
claim_text: Titanium is listed at roughly 8-15% of total mass for strength and durability.
source_document: AetherWave Colonisation Reference Tables (DaftMav-derived)
section_heading: Resource Requirements for Major Materials
planner_implication: Do not let titanium disappear under multi-economy cannibalization if you are building heavily.

title: Building Fabricators and Structural Regulators matter for surface construction
claim_text: Building Fabricators and Structural Regulators are called out as important specifically for surface installations.
source_document: AetherWave Colonisation Reference Tables (DaftMav-derived)
section_heading: Resource Requirements for Major Materials
planner_implication: Ground-heavy builds need these commodities planned explicitly.

title: Medium settlements are exactly double small settlement resources
claim_text: Medium settlements typically require exactly 2x the resources of small settlements of the same type.
source_document: AetherWave Colonisation Reference Tables (DaftMav-derived)
section_heading: Resource Requirements for Major Materials
planner_implication: Use exact 2x scaling when roughing out hauling plans for medium settlements.

title: Large settlements are exactly triple small settlement resources
claim_text: Large settlements typically require exactly 3x the resources of small settlements of the same type.
source_document: AetherWave Colonisation Reference Tables (DaftMav-derived)
section_heading: Resource Requirements for Major Materials
planner_implication: Use exact 3x scaling when roughing out hauling plans for large settlements.

title: Primary installation surcharge applies to the first installation
claim_text: The primary installation has a resource surcharge of +16% for Tier 1, +32% for Tier 2, and +20% for Tier 3.
source_document: AetherWave Colonisation Reference Tables (DaftMav-derived)
section_heading: Important Construction Notes
planner_implication: Add the surcharge to first-installation logistics estimates.

title: Settlement pad sizes can differ from their advertised sizes
claim_text: Many settlements advertise landing pad sizes that do not match what they actually provide in game.
source_document: AetherWave Colonisation Reference Tables (DaftMav-derived)
section_heading: Important Construction Notes
planner_implication: Validate actual pad access before recommending a facility to large haulers.

title: Large settlements are efficient green-CP generators
claim_text: Large settlements provide double the Tier 3 construction points of standard installations and are therefore more efficient for progression.
source_document: AetherWave Colonisation Reference Tables (DaftMav-derived)
section_heading: Important Construction Notes
planner_implication: Use large settlements when green-CP efficiency matters more than final facility mix.

title: Eirene satellite misbuilds as scientific installation
claim_text: The Eirene satellite variant is bugged and constructs as a Scientific Installation.
source_document: AetherWave Colonisation Reference Tables (DaftMav-derived)
section_heading: Important Construction Notes
planner_implication: Avoid Eirene if the intended outcome depends on getting the satellite class.

title: Ichnaea relay misbuilds as communications installation
claim_text: The Ichnaea relay variant is bugged and constructs as a Communications Installation.
source_document: AetherWave Colonisation Reference Tables (DaftMav-derived)
section_heading: Important Construction Notes
planner_implication: Avoid Ichnaea if the intended outcome depends on getting the relay class.
